pattern: Simulate the distribution rule; use coordinate compression plus a Fenwick tree to answer 'count strictly greater than val' in O(log n) per query.
hints: The rule is deterministic: simulate it step by step. The bottleneck is answering greaterCount(arr, val) quickly for each insertion.
Coordinate-compress all values in nums so they map to ranks 1..n, then maintain a Fenwick (BIT) tree per array. 'Count greater than val' becomes a suffix sum query.
Build one BIT per array. For each new element, query both BITs to compare suffix counts, append to the winning array, and update the corresponding BIT at the element's compressed rank.

edge_inputs.description: all equal elements, always tie-breaks into arr1 or arr2 by length
strictly increasing: greaterCount comparisons always favor arr2
tie on greaterCount but arr1 is longer so element goes to arr2
large distinct values testing coordinate compression
only three elements with first two equal

stress.description: large random array with values up to 1e9
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
